items.q: What is the function of the bridge in a pop song?
items.correct: To provide contrast before the final chorus, often by changing melody, key, or texture.
items.misconception: It is the same as the chorus.
items.random_wrong: It is the very first section.
items.why: Students confuse bridge with chorus because both are 'big' sections. The bridge appears once (usually after the second chorus) to provide CONTRAST and renew interest before returning.

items.q: How many crotchets are in a semibreve?
items.why: Students confuse the relative durations. A semibreve (whole note) = 2 minims = 4 crotchets = 8 quavers. Each level halves in length.

items.q: What is a tone row in serial music?
items.correct: An ordered series of all 12 chromatic pitches, used as the basis of a piece.
items.misconception: A row of notes played together as a chord.
items.random_wrong: Any sequence of notes a composer chooses.
items.why: Students miss the SPECIFIC structure. Schoenberg's 12-tone technique uses ALL 12 pitches in a fixed order before any repeats, generating melodies and harmonies from that row.

items.q: Which year did the Sex Pistols release 'Anarchy in the UK'?
items.why: It's 1976. “1972” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
It's Kick drum on every beat. “Four guitar players” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Approximately what BPM is typical for disco?
items.why: The real figure is 120. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
